title: envctl database-to-directory materialization defined
objective: Define envctl as the deterministic projection engine that reconstructs a selected PostgreSQL branch and pinned version into a clean, disposable directory or build input, with authority flowing one direction only (PostgreSQL canonical -> filesystem derived), no reverse ownership edge, and no manual edits to generated output surviving re-projection.

work_items.decision: Parameterize every projection by an explicit pinned selector (production_branch_id + version_snapshot_id + status='production'); resolution is by immutable identity, never mutable 'latest', so a selector always names the same row set. Grounded in Architecture Blueprint materialization step 1 and Context Matrix row 25.

Reconstruct the directory + module tree in memory purely from module_path metadata and causal dependency edges; the tree is a pure function of stored identities with no host path resolution. Verified by path-set hash equality and relationship-graph hash equality. Grounded in Architecture Blueprint materialization step 2 and section 5 (causal dependency edges).

Join ordered raw_code blocks into complete files at exact bytes with no reformatting; ordering derives from stored block ordinals. Verified by composed BlobStore SHA-256 equality. Adopts proven Exact-byte materialization (ADOPT-001, nu_plugin PR#16, materialization_paths.rs, 7 tests).

Apply stored file-mode metadata; enforce symlink limits and fail closed on a non-clean target; never read existing output as input. Verified by materialization_paths.rs mode/symlink checks. Records the open cross-OS permission-parity gap (Linux-fallback lanes only).

Capture module_path/language/status/mode metadata such that its projection is content-stable per version. Verified by captured_metadata_json_projection_is_stable. Adopts proven SHA-256 identity (ADOPT-001, nu_plugin PR#14, workspace_identity).

Define the repeat-projection parity method: project the pinned version twice and assert whole-tree SHA-256 workspace-identity equality. Live run against a provisioned PostgreSQL is an open dependency; the SHA-256 identity primitive is already green.

Emit a projection manifest mapping every emitted file to production_branch_id, version_snapshot_id, module_path, ordered block content hashes, file_mode, and composed_file_sha256; every hash resolves to a real PostgreSQL/BlobStore row. Adopts proven envctl export contract (ADOPT-001, nu_plugin PR#19, CDB035) and Cryptographic Witness Chain lineage.

Guarantee the output holds no authority: deletable with zero loss, regenerated wholesale from the pinned version, manual edits never merged and discarded on re-projection, and no write path back into PostgreSQL. Aligns with STORE-001 derived-projections class (write_authority = envctl projection read-only; recovery_source = re-projection) and the single-durable-writer invariant.

completion_gate: A selected database version deterministically reconstructs paths, bytes, metadata, ordering, permissions, and module relationships; repeated projections are byte-identical; projection output is disposable and traceable to database identities.
